(auto) ensure agent ends day at home

diary:
Woke to rain drumming against the windows—the kind of Tuesday that makes you want to stay under the covers. Fed my starter first thing, watched it bubble with life while the coffee brewed. The drive across to South Lake Union was slower than usual; everyone seemed confused about the bridge closure, but I'd checked the news early and took Aurora instead. Dodged the worst of it.

Service was solid today. Lunch moved smoothly through my station—I kept the plating tight, the rhythm steady. Grabbed a quiet break in the kitchen around 1:30, just sat with a bowl of broth and yesterday's bread. By dinner, my shoulders were tight but my hands were sure. The drizzle outside softened the evening somehow, made the dining room feel more intimate. Sent out maybe thirty covers cleanly. Felt good.
thought_process: The rain and bridge closure could've thrown me off—I know plenty of chefs who let external chaos seep into their prep. But I learned early on that the only thing I control is my station and my focus. I checked traffic before leaving home, which saved me maybe fifteen minutes of frustration. At lunch I made a conscious choice to slow down instead of rushing through—that half-hour of stillness recharged me for the dinner push. My mood stayed even because I didn't fight the day's rhythm; I worked with it. The rain outside actually helped, gave me something calm to push against while things got intense inside.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
